sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.28 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite group$G$is generated by a conjugacy class$D$of involutions and let$D_i=\{ d_1 d_i d_1, ,d_i$are different pairwise commuting elements of$D\}$. What is$G$, if$D_1, ,D_n$are all its different conjugacy classes of involutions? For example, the Fischer groups$F_{22}$and$F_{23}$satisfy this condition with$n=3$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
